input:
When they were ready, the Prophet moved in. Building the mosque was the first major step the Prophet took as he settled in Madinah. He also built a mosque at Qubāʿ, where he had stopped for a few days before completing his journey to Madinah. One wonders, therefore, why he attached such a great importance to mosque-building. The mosque is not simply a place for worship. Had it been so, it would have been given a secondary priority by the Prophet.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 15: Madinah

output.scene_title: Building the Prophet's Mosque in Madinah
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 15: Madinah

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a brown cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by brown cloak and subtle warm light.

output.moral_lesson: A place of worship is not just a physical structure, but a symbol of community and unity.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet began building his mosque in Madinah. It was an important step in establishing a community and a place for worship. He also built a mosque at Qubāʿ, where he had stopped before arriving in Madinah.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, standing amidst construction. Simple scene, no violence or conflict.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a 7th century Madinah mud-brick mosque under construction. The Prophet, a middle-aged man with a dignified posture, wearing a white thobe and brown cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing in front of a group of men with clay bricks and tools. The men are shown in the background, with some holding tools and others standing with their hands clasped together. Simple mud-brick walls, a few clay water jugs in the corner. Medium shot, eye level. Earthy warm palette. No modern objects.
